profile.bio: Innovative electronics engineer specializing in robotics, embedded systems, and mobile app development. Building cool stuff from Manila City, PH. 🐼
profile.ab1: Innovative Electronics Engineer with 8+ years of experience in electronics and robotics. Highly experienced in writing code and algorithms as well as building complex robotics designs through various programming languages.
profile.ab2: Possess an unbridled passion for building, tinkering and making stuff. Most of my projects were born out of pure curiosity during COVID lockdowns — and the making never stopped.

projects.title: QR Attendance System
projects.desc:
QR-SMS Attendance System
A desktop attendance management application built with C# and Windows Forms, designed to streamline student check-in/check-out for schools using QR code scanning and automated SMS notifications to guardians.
Overview
This project addresses a common pain point in school administration — manual attendance tracking is slow, error-prone, and leaves parents out of the loop. The QR-SMS Attendance System replaces that process with a real-time, camera-based solution that logs attendance and immediately notifies a student's guardian via SMS the moment they arrive or are dismissed.
Key Features
QR Code Scanning — Uses the device's webcam (via OpenCvSharp) to continuously scan for student QR codes in real time, with a live camera feed, bounding-box overlay on successful scans, and intelligent decode throttling to keep CPU usage low.
Student Database Integration — Loads a student roster directly from an Excel spreadsheet (.xlsx), automatically mapping columns for name, LRN, phone number, address, year level, section, track/strand, guardian, and more. No manual re-entry required.
Automated SMS via Webhook — On a successful scan, the app builds a personalized SMS message and dispatches it to a connected Android device via a webhook endpoint. Messages are fully customizable with template placeholders ({fullname}, {status}, {time}, etc.) and include a 5-second cooldown per student to prevent duplicate sends.
On-Time / Late Detection — Compares the scan timestamp against a configurable scheduled time (defaulting to 8:00 AM) and automatically stamps each log entry as On Time, Late, or Dismissed for OUT scans.
CSV Attendance Logging — Every scan is written to a persistent attendance.csv file with full student details, timestamp, and IN/OUT status — ready for reporting or further processing.
QR Code Generator — Built-in tool to generate and save QR codes from any text input, making it easy to produce ID QR codes for students directly within the same application.
Tech Stack

Language / Framework: C# · .NET · Windows Forms
Computer Vision: OpenCvSharp (webcam capture & frame processing)
QR Decoding / Encoding: ZXing.Net
Excel I/O: ClosedXML
Messaging: HTTP webhook integration (JSON payload to Android SMS bridge)
Data Storage: CSV (attendance log) · plain text (webhook URL history)
